name: Cubo de Don Sancho and La Sagrada — circular from Aldehuela de la Bóveda
name_original: Cubo de Don Sancho y La Sagrada — circular desde Aldehuela de la Bóveda
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Fuente De San Esteban, located in the Campo Charro region of central Salamanca, Spain, offer diverse terrain for cyclists. The area is characterized by expansive meadow landscapes and green forests, providing a tranquil setting for rides. Rural roads connect small towns, allowing for exploration away from main thoroughfares. The region also features unique rock formations, adding a distinctive visual element to the routes. Best road cycling routes around Fuente De San Esteban The most popular road cycling route is Campo Charro Loop from Tamames, a 44.2 miles (71.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 2 minutes to complete. This route traverses the scenic Campo Charro region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cubo de Don Sancho and La Sagrada — circular from Aldehuela de la Bóveda, a moderate 61.6 miles (99.1 km) path. This route offers views of the rural landscape and traditional villages. Local road cyclists also love the Miranda de Castañar – Ascent to Puerto de Valero loop from Vecinos, a 71.6 miles (115.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 5 hours 45 minutes. Road cycling around Fuente De San Esteban is defined by green forests, meadow landscapes, and rural roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 60 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Fuente De San Esteban's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect when road cycling around Fuente De San Esteban?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape characterized by expansive meadow landscapes and lush green forests. You'll often find yourself on tranquil, rural roads connecting small towns, providing a peaceful cycling experience. The area is also noted for unique rock formations, adding a distinctive visual element to your rides through the traditional Campo Charro region.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Fuente De San Esteban?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for beginners. Out of the 17 road cycling routes in the area, 2 are classified as easy. An example of a moderate route that could be approached by those building experience is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030768066/campo-charro-loop-from-tamames" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo Charro Loop from Tamames&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 71.2 km with moderate elevation.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes in the Fuente De San Esteban area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 4 difficult routes available. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda de Castañar – Ascent to Puerto de Valero loop from Vecinos&lt;/a&gt;, which is 115.3 km long and features significant elevation gain, often taking around 5 hours 45 minutes to complete. Another difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40012525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Valero – Miranda de Castañar loop from Tamames&lt;/a&gt;, covering 90.9 km with over 1600 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Fuente De San Esteban?
A: Yes, many of the routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026969317/cubo-de-don-sancho-and-la-sagrada-circular-from-aldehuela-de-la-boveda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubo de Don Sancho and La Sagrada — circular from Aldehuela de la Bóveda&lt;/a&gt; is a 99.1 km loop offering views of rural landscapes and traditional villages.

Q: What historical sites or natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region offers several points of interest. You can explore the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2962040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cruz de Navagallega Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which provides beautiful views. The area also features unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4087092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa Pastures and Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, a route includes a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1491056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Frades de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to explore small towns in the southern Castilian plateau.

Q: Are there options for longer distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. For those looking for longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda de Castañar – Ascent to Puerto de Valero loop from Vecinos&lt;/a&gt; extends for 115.3 km. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026969317/cubo-de-don-sancho-and-la-sagrada-circular-from-aldehuela-de-la-boveda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubo de Don Sancho and La Sagrada — circular from Aldehuela de la Bóveda&lt;/a&gt;, which is 99.1 km long.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Fuente De San Esteban?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, the region's central Salamanca location suggests pleasant conditions for cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winter might bring cooler temperatures.

Q: Can I find places to stop for refreshments or explore small towns along the routes?
A: Yes, the rural roads around Fuente De San Esteban connect various small towns and villages. This provides opportunities to explore local culture and find places for refreshments. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030768066/campo-charro-loop-from-tamames" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo Charro Loop from Tamames&lt;/a&gt; starts and ends in Tamames, a village with historical significance, where you can find amenities.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Fuente De San Esteban?
A: Road cyclists using komoot frequently praise the region's tranquil setting, characterized by green forests and expansive meadow landscapes. The quiet rural roads, which allow for exploration away from main thoroughfares, are also a highlight. Many appreciate the diverse terrain and the unique rock formations that add visual interest to their rides.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: While dedicated 'family-friendly' road cycling routes aren't explicitly categorized, the 2 easy-rated routes in the area could be suitable for families with older children or those looking for a more relaxed pace. These routes typically feature less challenging terrain and elevation, providing a gentler introduction to road cycling in the region.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on road cycling routes around Fuente De San Esteban?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly depending on the route's difficulty. Easy routes will have minimal climbs, while moderate routes can feature several hundred meters of ascent, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14880089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tamames Tower – Cilleros de la Bastida loop from Puebla de Yeltes&lt;/a&gt; with over 730 meters of climbing. Difficult routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda de Castañar – Ascent to Puerto de Valero loop from Vecinos&lt;/a&gt;, can involve over 1800 meters of elevation gain.
tips: La Sagrada is a Spanish municipality and town in the province of Salamanca. In the town is the Parochial Church of San Miguel and a Monument in memory of the Muñoz level crossing accident in 1978. The schoolchildren of the municipality, who were traveling in the school bus that was hit by a locomotive and that is why a statue in his memory.

The municipality of El Cubo has been inhabited since the Iron Age and the Calzada de los Mártires that passes through it attests to the Roman presence there. In addition, the Huebra river crosses this municipal term in its northern part!

Garcirrey is a Spanish municipality and town in the province of Salamanca. Its municipal term is made up of the towns of Alcornocal, Ardonsillero, Berrocalejo, Casasola, Garcirrey, Moral de Castro, Valdelama, Vilvis and Villarejo, the latter depopulated.
